findings.description: FUSE drift: build-scripts/generate-html.js workspace md5=2556e983 differs from github fa443f14. Single-line diff in a ds-vb-caption template that includes '+ 1 analyst-added claim (WIN-070)' on workspace but not on github main.
findings.evidence: Workspace mtime 2026-05-18 05:38; github head commit 2026-05-18T16:54Z REPAIR (workspace-sync abort-cycle restoration). docs/index.html (the rendered output) does NOT contain the 'analyst-added claim' phrase in either workspace or github — the diverged template code is dormant (not yet exercised by a build.js publish). Reproduction: `diff build-scripts/generate-html.js /tmp/gh-gen-html.js` returns the single line difference at L500.
findings.interpretation: REPAIR commit likely restored a pre-divergence version, losing the WIN-070-caption addition that had been edited into workspace during the 2026-05-15 'EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1589' decider run. The lost change is cosmetic (caption only); current rendered HTML is unaffected. Next build.js publish from operator clone will re-emit using github's caption (without WIN-070 mention) and overwrite workspace's generate-html.js back to fa443f14 — self-healing.
findings.recommended_action: monitor; no PROP needed. Re-check next Mode 2 run; if still diverged after another decider commit cycle, escalate to PROP for explicit reconciliation.

findings.description: Decider daily-report filename template substitution bug — one occurrence: monitor/decisions/daily-report-2026-05-18T2026-05-18T05-25.json. The date stem was concatenated twice between the literal 'daily-report-' prefix and the 'HH-MM.json' suffix, producing 'YYYY-MM-DDTYYYY-MM-DDTHH-MM.json' instead of 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH-MM.json'.
findings.evidence: File run_id='decider-2026-05-18T05-20', run_at='2026-05-18T05:25:27Z'. Content is a valid daily report (mode=bau, 2 patches, 1 issue, 1 closure). Filename grep across monitor/ shows this is the only instance of the pattern in ~30 decider runs.
findings.interpretation: Probable cause: an interpolation like `daily-report-${date}T${date}T${time}.json` where ${date}=YYYY-MM-DD got expanded twice. Single occurrence in ~30 runs = ~3% incidence — below useful-PROP threshold for now. Worth a watch-list note.
findings.recommended_action: Watch for recurrence over next ~50 decider runs. If incidence stays at ~3% or below, no PROP needed (operator can rename manually if it bothers them; FUSE cannot unlink so retention is benign). If recurrence climbs above ~5% (≥2-3 more cases this week), file a PROP to identify the template-substitution call-site in decider.md / decider-reporting.md.

findings.description: ISS-2015 has NaN age in the age-of-open-issues computation because it carries a `found_date='2026-05-09'` field rather than `found_at`/`created_at` ISO timestamps. The dispatcher's standard ageDays() helper checks (found_at || created_at) and returns null for ISS-2015.
findings.evidence: Snippet from open-issues.json shows ISS-2015 has found_date but no found_at/created_at. dispatcher's pre-flight metric correctly skips it (returns null → not counted in age_ge_14d / age_ge_30d). Other reporting paths that don't have the null-guard show 'NaNd'.
findings.interpretation: ISS-2015 is a 2026-05-14 renamed item (renamed_from ISS-1921; rename_reason: ID collision in both open and closed). Original ISS-1921 may have used the old found_date schema. Effect on triage: minimal (other fields fill in via fixed_at). Effect on tinker reporting: cosmetic NaN.
findings.recommended_action: No PROP. Either (a) decider can backfill found_at = found_date+'T00:00:00Z' when renaming ISO-incompatible items, or (b) tinker age helper can fall back to found_date when found_at missing. Both are 1-line fixes; defer to next Mode 4 run if needed.

findings.description: Two cycle-cycle filename duplicates in curmudgeon reviews: WIN-067.c3 appears twice and WIN-003.c2 appears twice. Most likely a benign concurrent clone-vs-FUSE write race (a single curmudgeon review file landing under two near-identical names) — not a re-review.
findings.evidence: `ls monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/ | grep -oE 'WIN-[0-9]+\.c[0-9]+' | sort | uniq -c` returns 2x for WIN-067.c3 and WIN-003.c2; all other patterns ≤1. Both pairs likely have nearly-identical content (one is the clone-side commit and one is the FUSE write).
findings.interpretation: Pre-existing — not a new pattern. Curmudgeon's intake already dedupes target_id but file-level dedup is not enforced. Low impact: no double-payment, no analyst confusion (reviewer reads the latest by mtime, and content is near-identical).
findings.recommended_action: Note in next Mode 3 — if count grows, write a PROP for filename uniqueness check in curmudgeon write path. Currently 2 dupes out of ~250 reviews = <1% — below threshold.
